# Red Door Property Management

> Red Door Property Management provides residential property management services in Great Falls and North Central Montana. The company is built around three measurable pillars: Speed to Lease, Higher Rent, and Maintenance Excellence — designed to maximize owner income, minimize vacancy, and protect long-term property value.

Red Door serves landlords with portfolios of all sizes, from single-property owners to multi-property investors. Professional management is positioned not as a cost, but as a net income improvement through faster leasing, stronger rents, and efficient maintenance coordination. All pricing is transparent with no hidden fees or maintenance markups.

## Core Pillars

- **Speed to Lease**: Minimizing vacancy through professional marketing, photography, listing optimization, showing coordination, application processing, and proactive lead management.
- **Higher Rent**: Data-driven pricing strategy, market positioning, renewal negotiations, and rent increase implementation aligned with market conditions.
- **Maintenance Excellence**: 24/7 maintenance coordination, vendor dispatch, repair oversight, preventative planning, and tenant accountability for damages.

## Services

### Leasing
- Professional property marketing and photography
- Market rent analysis and listing optimization
- Showing coordination and application processing
- Lease preparation and execution

### Tenant Screening
- Verified stable income
- Rental history and landlord references
- Background checks
- Credit evaluation

### Financial & Administrative
- Rent collection and owner disbursements
- Monthly financial statements and year-end reporting
- Lease compliance enforcement
- Montana landlord-tenant law compliance
- Notice delivery and documentation

### Owner Experience
- Centralized communication and performance visibility
- Reduced owner involvement in daily operations
- Professional handling of difficult tenant conversations

## Service Area

Great Falls, Cascade County, Sun Prairie, Choteau, Fort Benton, Wolf Creek, Conrad, Shelby, Cut Bank, Havre, and Chester — covering North Central Montana and the Hi-Line. All locations are managed through the Great Falls office at 202 2nd Ave S, Ste 201, Great Falls, MT 59401.

## Ideal Clients

- Residential landlords with 1–5 properties seeking passive income
- Owners transitioning from self-management to professional management
- Investors who want to focus on strategy rather than daily operations

## Key Differentiators

- Performance measured by outcomes (vacancy rate, rent achieved, maintenance speed) rather than promotional claims
- Transparent pricing with no maintenance markups
- Tenant screening process supports long-term property protection by placing financially qualified, responsible residents
- Designed to help owners answer: *Do I want to be a property manager, or do I want to be an investor?*

## FAQ

### How do I choose a property manager for my rental property?
Evaluate managers based on outcomes rather than promises. The most important questions are whether a company can consistently reduce vacancy, achieve strong rental pricing, and protect the property long term. Red Door Property Management encourages owners to assess Speed to Lease, marketing quality, and maintenance efficiency. In the Great Falls market, delays in leasing or poor maintenance coordination can quickly erode profitability.

### What makes a property management company top-rated in Montana?
Top-rated companies deliver measurable financial outcomes: faster leasing timelines that reduce lost income, higher achievable rents through professional marketing, and maintenance systems that keep tenants satisfied while protecting the property. Landlords across Great Falls and Cascade County value predictable performance over promotional claims.

### Do you work with small rental portfolios?
Yes. Red Door Property Management works with many owners who have only one to five properties. By improving Speed to Lease and achieving Higher Rent, professional management often increases total owner profit even after fees. Transparent pricing without maintenance markups ensures owners understand exactly where value is created.

### How do you evaluate a property management company's reliability?
Ask how quickly vacancies are filled, whether rents are supported by market data, and how fast maintenance issues are resolved. Reliable property managers produce consistent outcomes through systems, not one-off effort. Red Door tracks performance around all three pillars to give owners predictable results.

### What is involved in tenant screening?
Red Door uses a structured screening process evaluating verified stable income, rental history and landlord references, background checks, and credit. This supports Maintenance Excellence by placing tenants who are financially qualified and more likely to care for the home, reducing turnover, late payments, and unnecessary property wear.

### What are the alternatives to self-managing my rental?
Many landlords begin by self-managing but over time find it involves late-night maintenance calls, weekend emergencies, and coordinating repairs while traveling or spending time with family. Red Door helps owners shift from active property management to passive investing by handling leasing, communication, and maintenance coordination. The core question is: do you want to be a property manager, or an investor?

### What does a property management company actually do?
A professional property manager handles nearly every operational aspect of owning rental property. Red Door provides marketing, tenant screening, lease preparation, rent collection, maintenance coordination, vendor management, financial reporting, lease compliance, and Montana landlord-tenant law compliance — so owners can focus on investment strategy instead of daily problems.

### How does Red Door help landlords achieve higher rent?
Through data-driven pricing strategy, market positioning recommendations, renewal negotiations, and rent increase implementation aligned with market conditions. Tenant retention strategies also reduce turnover costs, which directly improves net income.

### What does Red Door's onboarding process look like?
The process has four stages. First, an initial client meeting covering how Red Door operates, your property details, and your objectives. Second, a pre-signing phase including initial documents and a 15-minute follow-up. Third, contract review, property upload, and owner portal setup. Fourth, professional photography and listing across 20+ rental platforms. Ongoing service includes monthly rent roll and owner statements, plus quarterly photo inspections with recommended repairs.

### What are Red Door's management fees?
Pricing is transparent and publicly available. Long-term residential management is 10% of monthly rent collected (or $85 per unit, whichever is greater), plus a $20/unit/month technology and operations fee. Short-term and mid-term rentals are 15% of gross revenue, plus a $50/unit/month STR technology fee. A one-time $100 property setup fee applies. Leasing and tenant screening is $199 per lease event. Discounts are available for military, first responders, and multi-unit portfolios.

### What areas does Red Door serve?
Red Door is based in Great Falls and serves a broad area of North Central Montana and the Hi-Line, including: Great Falls, Cascade County, Sun Prairie, Choteau, Fort Benton, Wolf Creek, Conrad (Pondera County), Shelby (Toole County), Cut Bank (Glacier County), Havre (Hill County), and Chester (Liberty County), as well as surrounding Hi-Line communities. All locations are managed through the Great Falls office.

### Why should I choose Red Door over other property management companies?
Red Door offers four core advantages: local expertise (the team lives and works in Central Montana), a marketing and leasing system that reduces vacancy and places quality tenants with a low eviction rate, proactive maintenance and quarterly inspections that protect asset value, and a strong communication standard so owners are never left wondering about the status of their property.

### What if I'm a frustrated landlord or burned out from self-managing?
Red Door specifically works with landlords who are new to ownership, dealing with poor service from a prior manager, or exhausted from self-managing. The team handles tax implications, maintenance, Fair Housing law compliance, and day-to-day operations so owners can focus on their family, financial goals, and the passive income real estate investing is meant to provide.

### Does Red Door offer a satisfaction guarantee?
Yes. Red Door offers a 100% satisfaction guarantee. If at any time an owner feels expectations have not been met, they may cancel their contract with 30 days written notice — no penalties or fine print.
